title: Stizolobium platyphyllum
taxonomy: Stizolobium platyphyllum is a species of flowering plant in the legume family, Fabaceae. It belongs to the genus Stizolobium, which is part of the tribe Dalbergieae. The plant is native to tropical regions and is known for its distinctive leaf structure. The classification of S. platyphyllum places it within the subfamily Faboideae, which includes many other legume species. Further classification includes its order and family, with S. platyphyllum being a part of the order Fabales.

intro: Stizolobium platyphyllum, also known as the "sleepy vine" or "sleepy night", is a plant species in the Fabaceae family, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The health benefits of Stizolobium platyphyllum include its sedative and anxiolytic properties, which can help alleviate insomnia and anxiety, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In herbal medicine, Stizolobium platyphyllum is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs to promote restful sleep and reduce stress.
